summary.intent: Fix a biome formatting failure, cut a new release (20260328.03), respond to a GitHub discussion about forum topic isolation, and fix a failing e2e test asserting session list visibility when vault is sealed.
summary.outcome: All four goals completed: biome fix committed and pushed, release 20260328.03 tagged and pushed, discussion comment posted, and e2e test fixed by changing the locator from the hidden modal's chat-session-name span to the sidebar's .session-item[data-session-key].

repo: Release tags follow a date-based scheme (YYYYMMDD.NN); the prepare script auto-increments the suffix and updates the changelog.
The release prep commit can be pushed to remote without the tag; tagging and pushing separately is valid recovery when the tag was deleted locally.

session-header.js is mounted twice: once in the toolbar (showName=false) and once inside #chatMoreModal (hidden by default), so getByText('Main chat') resolves to hidden elements in the modal, not the visible sidebar.
The sidebar session item renders label via <span data-label-text> inside .session-item[data-session-key], which is the correct locator for asserting session visibility in e2e tests.
biome formatting is enforced via CI and the local-validate.sh script; long console.warn() calls must be broken across multiple lines.

code.finding: SessionHeader is mounted twice: toolbar mount passes showName=false (header text hidden), modal mount is inside #chatMoreModal (hidden by default). Both instances of .chat-session-name are hidden, making getByText() on that class unreliable in e2e tests.

Test previously used getByText('Main chat', {exact:true}).toBeVisible() which matched hidden modal elements. Fixed to use .locator('.session-item[data-session-key="main"]').first() to target the visible sidebar item.
Biome requires multi-line formatting for console.warn() calls with more than one argument when line length exceeds the limit.

workflow: git rebase with stash can leave the repo in an ambiguous state (rebase completed but stash pop left unstaged conflicts); always check git status carefully before proceeding.
When a release prep commit lands on remote but the tag was deleted locally, the cleanest recovery is to re-tag the existing commit rather than re-running the prepare script.
The local-validate.sh script on macOS forces non-CUDA paths and can be overridden via LOCAL_VALIDATE_* env vars.
biome --write does not always persist when run via Bash tool in the session; directly editing the file is more reliable.

friction: biome --write appeared to succeed in-session but the change wasn't persisted, requiring a manual Edit to apply the fix.
Release prep was interrupted mid-rebase, leaving the repo with staged/unstaged conflicts and a partially applied stash that required multiple recovery steps.
The release tag was deleted locally after a failed push attempt, requiring a separate tag+push step to complete the release.
git pull was blocked by unstaged changes to apps/ios/GraphQL/Schema/schema.graphqls mid-session.
The failing e2e test required extensive investigation (multiple grep passes across session-header, session-list, CSS, and page-chat) before the root cause (hidden modal mount) was identified.
open_items: The apps/ios/GraphQL/Schema/schema.graphqls change (adding kind, warnings, ContextWarning fields to ContextFile) was present as an unstaged modification throughout the session but was committed without explicit discussion — may need iOS client updates.
The release workflow relies on the prepare script running cleanly before pushing; there is no guard against pushing a release prep commit without a corresponding tag.

The e2e test for 'sealed vault does not hide unencrypted session list' may still be fragile if the sidebar session label race condition (seedSessionsFromGon vs fetchSessions) causes 'main' to appear instead of 'Main chat' in the sidebar.
